novelty_flags.label: Related work / Conjecture: uniform honesty and upper length
novelty_flags.one_line: Add ArmstrongKolesar2020SimpleHonest: its bias-aware honest-CI analysis and its warning against data-driven smoothness adaptation are a closer published comparator for the proposed union-honesty claim than the current survey acknowledges.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Conjecture: two-regime candidate
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The separated/nonseparated rate display is explicitly conditional on the unproved union-honesty upper construction and is not a claimed minimax characterization.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Treat the rate diagram only as a candidate conditional on Conjecture: uniform honesty and upper length; the settled lower-bound theorem remains independent.

source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The article proposes a smoothed robust estimator and asymptotically accurate resampling inference for both the optimal-regime parameter and optimal value function.
The article gives subagging confidence intervals with nominal coverage for the optimal value and reports average-length comparisons.
The preprint develops adaptive smoothing, valid inference under nonregularity, and an asymptotic-variance lower bound within a robust asymptotically linear unbiased estimator class.
The preprint constructs a constant-nuisance-fit softmax-smoothed optimal-policy-value estimator, avoiding parametric restrictions and strong nonresponse-excluding margin assumptions.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Armstrong and Kolesar (2020) and explicitly distinguish the finite predeclared union from unknown smoothness adaptation; state which known-smoothness quantities are fixed before sampling and which are selected by the interval.

Strengthen the main conjecture into a stratumwise upper-versus-testing-modulus comparison, including an explicit condition under which the candidate interval attains the ordered-modulus rate up to constants or incurs a quantified adaptation penalty.

Add a published-competitor boundary corollary comparing the interval directly with the smoothing procedures of Xu--Guo and Whitehouse--Chen--Austern--Syrgkanis on a common randomized binary-treatment subclass.

recommended_next_step: Revise the literature positioning around honest smoothness adaptation, then retain this angle as a field-tier conditional optimal-value inference program.
